title: Kintaro

{{Quote|That's Grand Moff Kintaro's sector, is it not? He has been lax in allowing such a base to become established. We will speak to him.|Emperor Palpatine|<ref name="SOTE novel">[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (novel)|''Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire'' novel]]</ref>}}
'''Kintaro''' was one of the first twenty [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moffs]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], ruling over the [[Sarin Oversector]] as its regional [[Governor/Legends|governor]] from [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]] until his [[Death/Legends|death]] around [[3 ABY/Legends|3.5 ABY]]. Kintaro was first appointed a [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] under the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] during the final [[Standard day/Legends|days]] of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] as a result of [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] [[Sector Governance Decree/Legends|Sector Governance Decree]], which placed twenty military governors in command of the Republic's twenty [[Sector Army/Legends|Sector Armies]] to maximize the war effort. Supporting the Republic's [[Fifteenth Army]] in the Sarin [[Oversector/Legends|Oversector]], Kintaro maintained his military territory as Palpatine [[Declaration of a New Order|reorganized]] the Republic into the Empire and declared himself [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]]. Kintaro was soon elevated to Grand Moff and eventually established his [[Capital/Legends|capital]] on [[Talofan]].

During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Kintaro contributed to his own downfall by allowing the criminal venture [[Ororo Transportation/Legends|Ororo Transportation]] to operate a large-scale [[Spice/Legends|spice]] [[Merchant/Legends|trade]] in his territory in exchange for hefty kickbacks. Unbeknownst to Kintaro, Ororo's secret [[Vergesso Base|shipyard]] in the [[Bajic sector/Legends|Bajic sector]], which the Grand Moff helped to conceal, doubled as a sizable [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] safe [[Spaceport/Legends|port]]. When the [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] learned of the Rebel base from [[Dark Prince|Prince]] [[Xizor/Legends|Xizor]] of the [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]] crime syndicate, Ororo's major rival, shortly before the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], he ordered it [[Battle of the Bajic Shipyards|destroyed]] and had Kintaro executed for failing to secure his borders against Rebel incursion.

==Biography==
===The first Grand Moffs===
{{Quote|The regional governors now have direct control over their territories.|Grand Moff [[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Wilhuff Tarkin]]|audio=Tarkin-RegionalGovernors.flac|<ref name="Episode 4">{{Film|IV}}</ref>}}
The [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] prosecuted the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] against the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatist]] rebellion by dividing its [[Republic Military/Legends|armed forces]] into twenty [[Sector Army/Legends|Sector Armies]], each designed to protect a specific [[Oversector/Legends|oversector]], or priority [[Sector/Legends|sector]], of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].<ref name="Essential Guide to Warfare" /> Near the conclusion of the war,<ref name="ROTS novel">[[Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith (novelization)|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'' novelization]]</ref> in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]],<ref name="Essential Reader's Companion">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''</ref> [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] enacted the [[Sector Governance Decree/Legends|Sector Governance Decree]], which created a system of<ref name="Essential Atlas">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> twenty of the most powerful regional [[Governor/Legends|governors]],<ref name="Essential Guide to Warfare" /> called [[Moff/Legends|Moffs]], who were placed in command of each of the twenty Sector [[Army/Legends|Armies]] and their respective territories to maximize the Republic's war effort. This command structure allowed the Moffs to act without needing to consult with squabbling [[Senator/Legends|senators]].<ref name="Essential Atlas" /> Moff Kintaro was given the responsibility of supporting the Republic's [[Fifteenth Army]], also known as [[Hook Nebula/Legends|Hook Nebula]] Command, in the [[Sarin Oversector]], with sector headquarters at [[Qeimet system/Legends|Qeimet]]. The Fifteenth Army was envisioned as primarily a reserve force to reinforce Republic [[Troop/Legends|troops]] fighting on the [[Corellian Run/Legends|Corellian Run]], [[Hydian Way/Legends|Hydian Way]], and [[Rimma Trade Route/Legends|Rimma Trade Route]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlanes]].<ref name="Essential Guide to Warfare" />

[[File:SarinOversector.png|thumb|right|228px|The Sarin Oversector, Grand Moff Kintaro's power base]]
After the war's end, as the victorious Republic [[Declaration of a New Order|transitioned]] into the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] under the authority of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] Palpatine, the Sector Armies and the Moffs' military territories remained.<ref name="Essential Guide to Warfare" /> The Empire's militarization eventually reorganized the Republic's wartime boundaries into a more efficient and permanent set of oversectors, and the twenty original Republic Moffs became the first [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moffs]] of the Empire.<ref name="Essential Atlas" /> In the face of a largely superficial [[Imperial Senate/Legends|Imperial Senate]] that wielded no real legislative power or military control, the responsibility fell to Kintaro,<ref name="Essential Guide to Warfare" /> the Imperial [[High Commander]] of the Sarin Oversector,<ref name="SOTE Planets Guide">''[[Shadows of the Empire Planets Guide]]''</ref> and the other Grand Moffs to maintain peace, coordinate the vast resources of the [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial Military]], and ensure the doctrines of Palpatine's [[New Order/Legends|New Order]].<ref name="Essential Guide to Warfare" /> The [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] ultimately [[Dissolution of the Imperial Senate/Legends|dissolved the Senate]] in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], granting the Grand Moffs direct control over their territories.<ref name="New Essential Chronology">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref>

===Corruption in the Bajic sector===
Kintaro's sovereignty over the Sarin Oversector continued into the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]<ref name="SOTE Sourcebook">''[[Shadows of the Empire Sourcebook]]''</ref> and the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],<ref name="Date">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the events of ''[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire]]'' to [[3 ABY/Legends|3.5 ABY]].</ref> by which [[Time/Legends|time]] he had established his [[Capital/Legends|capital]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Talofan]]. The Grand Moff also allowed a lucrative and illicit [[Spice/Legends|spice]] [[Merchant/Legends|trade]] to operate within his domain. [[Ororo Transportation/Legends|Ororo Transportation]], a front company for the criminal [[Tenloss Syndicate/Legends|Tenloss Syndicate]], built a large, hidden [[Vergesso Base|shipyard]] in the remote [[Vergesso Asteroids/Legends|Vergesso Asteroids]], within the [[Lybeya system/Legends|Lybeya system]] of the [[Bajic sector/Legends|Bajic sector]].<ref name="SOTE Sourcebook" /> The [[Shipyard/Legends|facility]], one of the largest [[Shadowport/Legends|shadowports]] in the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]],<ref name="SOTE Planets Guide" /> served as the main distribution, storage, and repair hub for the Tenloss Syndicate's spice trade and other illicit dealings,<ref name="SOTE Sourcebook" /> catering to thousands of [[Metric ton/Legends|tons]] of illegal cargo each year.<ref name="SOTE Planets Guide" />

Indeed, Kintaro's sector authorities were well aware of the hidden Ororo base,<ref name="SOTE Sourcebook" /> but the Grand Moff deemed that it posed no threat to sector security, because he relied on the operation to supplement his income.<ref name="SOTE Planets Guide" /> Ororo paid Kintaro well for his silence, delivering a hefty [[Standard month/Legends|monthly]] tribute to his capital on Talofan. Kintaro was thereby satisfied with overlooking and even going so far as to conceal the operation from his own staff and [[Imperial officer/Legends|officers]]. He refrained from bringing the base to the attention of Imperial Military forces<ref name="SOTE Sourcebook" /> and ordered the local [[Imperial Security Bureau/Legends|Imperial Security Bureau]] to ignore it. The ISB, in turn, gladly focused its resources on uncovering [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] bases and Imperial traitors elsewhere.<ref name="SOTE Planets Guide" />

Kintaro and the ISB did not know, however, that the Ororo base also served as a major safe [[Spaceport/Legends|port]] for the Rebel Alliance and that much of the contraband that passed through the shipyard ended up in Rebel hands.<ref name="SOTE Sourcebook" /> Many types of Rebel and other [[Starship/Legends|starships]], from [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] to [[Cruiser/Legends|cruisers]], underwent repairs at the base right under Kintaro's nose.<ref name="CSWE-Vergesso">{{CSWECite|III|303|Vergesso asteroids}}</ref> Ororo developed sympathies for the Rebellion after too many encounters with the might of the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] and privately wished to see the Empire replaced with a government that would prove less efficient in hampering its criminal operations.<ref name="SOTE Sourcebook" /> In 3.5 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|ABY]],<ref name="Date" /> Ororo's growing spice business came to the attention of [[Dark Prince|Prince]] [[Xizor/Legends|Xizor]], [[Underlord|leader]] of the [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]] criminal organization, a major competitor of the Tenloss Syndicate.<ref name="CSWE-Vergesso" /> To both damage his rival and curry favor with the Emperor, Xizor informed the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] of the Ororo shipyard. The Emperor, displeased with Kintaro for allowing such a Rebel base to operate under his watch, ordered Vader to destroy the shipyard.<ref name="SOTE novel" />

The ensuing [[Battle of the Bajic Shipyards]] resulted in a tremendous Imperial victory,<ref name="SOTE novel" /> as Vader's forces destroyed hundreds of Rebel ships and thousands of [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] and crew members.<ref name="CSWE-Vergesso" /> Meanwhile, the Emperor had Kintaro [[Death/Legends|executed]] for failing to secure his territory against Rebel invasion.<ref name="CSWE-Kintaro" />

==Personality and traits==
Grand Moff Kintaro's corruption proved to be his undoing as the Imperial governor of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]' Bajic sector,<ref name="CSWE-Kintaro" /><ref name="SOTE Sourcebook" /> a hotbed of illegal spice trading among both the Black Sun and Tenloss Syndicate criminal factions.<ref name="CSWE-Bajic">{{CSWECite|I|55|Bajic sector}}</ref> In accepting bribes to allow Ororo Transportation to operate its spice trade within his territory, Kintaro failed to realize that Ororo's Vergesso [[Asteroid/Legends|Asteroid]] base also catered heavily to the Rebel Alliance—not that he was likely to investigate either so long as his lavish kickbacks continued.<ref name="SOTE Planets Guide" /> Kintaro's private arrangement with Ororo Transportation unwittingly left him vulnerable to the machinations of [[Prince/Legends|Prince]] Xizor of Black Sun,<ref name="SOTE novel" /><ref name="SOTE Sourcebook" /> who engineered the shipyard's destruction through the Emperor, thereby sealing Kintaro's fate, as vengeance against Ororo for daring to impede upon Black Sun's spice operations in that sector.<ref name="SOTE novel" />

==Behind the scenes==
Grand Moff Kintaro was first mentioned in passing in the [[1996]] novel [[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (novel)|''Shadows of the Empire'']], written by [[Steve Perry]].<ref name="SOTE novel" />

==Appearances==
